criteria.description: Tree.Lookup's doc comment no longer makes an unqualified 'never locks' claim
criteria.evidence: engine/btree/lookup.go lines 347-353: lock-free guarantee is explicitly scoped to 'the per-node latch level (NodeStore.Lock/TryLock in latch.go)', not to all locking

criteria.description: Doc comment accurately describes the actual locking behavior: a single, brief rootMu acquisition per attempt
criteria.evidence: engine/btree/lookup.go lines 357-365: dedicated paragraph starting 'One narrow, intentional exception: this function's retry loop calls t.Root() on every attempt ... which briefly acquires rootMu ... held only for the duration of a single map-free field read'; introduced by commit 3ef7cde

criteria.description: Tree.Lookup's implementation actually calls t.Root() (rootMu) on each retry attempt, confirming the doc comment is not merely asserted but true
criteria.evidence: engine/btree/lookup.go, func (t *Tree) Lookup (line 380): retry loop calls t.Root() every iteration
gofmt -l engine/btree/*.go -> no output (no files need formatting)

criteria.description: Test spec explicitly states doc-only change, no behavioral test required
criteria.evidence: Per issue #38 subtask 4.5.1.4 test spec verbatim: 'doc-only change; go vet ./engine/btree/... and gofmt -l clean, no behavioral test required.' No new test added, consistent with spec.

action_taken: no source change; docs-only confirmation, consistent with this milestone's established already-fixed re-confirm pattern
